scratchpads.name: Nexus
scratchpads.content:
# Nexus Client GUI Audit (v3.0-alpha5)

Audited 4219 lines across 27 .jsx/.js/.css files.

## Dead Code (delete or wire up — pick one)
- **Scratchpad.jsx** (284 lines) — fully functional, NOT in App.jsx, SearchModal links to it but nav fails silently
- **Ledger.jsx** (140 lines) — fully functional, NOT in App.jsx, merged into Graph but file remains
- **UsageGauge.jsx** (102 lines) — explicitly removed from Sidebar, orphaned
- **ActivityHeatmap.jsx** (162 lines) — never imported anywhere

Decision: delete UsageGauge + ActivityHeatmap (replaced by Fuel module).
For Scratchpad/Ledger: either re-add as hidden tabs or delete the .jsx files.

## CLI/UI Asymmetry — 9 server features have ZERO client UI
1. /api/thoughts (Thought Stack)
2. /api/critique (Self-Critique)
3. /api/guard (Decision Guard)
4. /api/advice (Advice Journal)
5. /api/predict (Predictive Tasks)
6. /api/plan (Autonomous Plan)
7. /api/auto-summary (Overseer auto-writes)
8. /api/impact/forecast (AI migration assessments)

The CLI has 54 commands. The dashboard exposes maybe 20% of them.

## Critical Bugs
- **App.jsx**: SearchModal navigates to scratchpad module that does not exist — silent failure
- **Sidebar.jsx**: ICON_MAP missing terminal entry — Terminal nav item shows blank icon
- **No ErrorBoundary**: any module crash takes down the whole app
- **MissionBoard.jsx**: drag-and-drop visual feedback never wires through onDragStart prop
- **Fuel.jsx**: doesn't destructure ws in signature, breaking real-time updates

## Cross-Cutting Debt
- **Inconsistent fetch patterns**: 6+ components use raw fetch() instead of api.* helpers
- **useApi.js**: missing 15+ endpoints (no thoughts, critique, guard, advice, predict, plan, auto-summary, forecast, heatmap, clock, digest)
- **WebSocket under-used**: Pulse polls every 10s, Fuel every 30s, ClockWidget every 30s — should subscribe to push events
- **Terminal.jsx** creates its OWN WebSocket instead of using useWebSocket hook
- **No error toasts**: failed API calls fail silently across all modules
- **No loading skeletons**: full-screen compass spinner is jarring

## Mobile / Responsive
- **Sidebar.jsx**: w-56 fixed, no hamburger toggle on <768px (eats 30% of mobile width)
- **Fuel.jsx**: hardcoded grid-cols-2, never collapses
- **Pulse.jsx**: lg:grid-cols-4 breaks badly at 1280px with sidebar
- **MissionBoard.jsx**: drag-and-drop doesn't work on touch devices, no fallback

## Accessibility Gaps
- Compass spinners have no aria-label (3+ places)
- Color-only status indicators (gauges) — no patterns for colorblind
- Modal focus traps don't restore to trigger button
- No skip links in Sidebar
- Sessions expand button has no aria-expanded

## Top 5 Fixes (do these first)
1. **Wire missing modules + delete dead code** — 30 min — unblocks Scratchpad/Ledger or removes them cleanly
2. **Consolidate fetch into useApi.js** — 45 min — fixes silent failures + adds 15 missing endpoints
3. **Replace polling with WebSocket subscriptions** — 60 min — Pulse/Fuel/ClockWidget all should push
4. **Mobile responsiveness** — 40 min — hamburger sidebar + responsive grids
5. **ErrorBoundary + toast on failures** — 50 min — eliminates silent crashes

## Top 5 Enhancements (after fixes)
1. **Build Thought Stack module** — 90 min — first new feature with no UI
2. **Add filter/sort to ActivityStream + Sessions** — 45 min — currently flat lists are unusable past 100 entries
3. **Wire Decision Guard into MissionBoard** — 60 min — warn on duplicate task creation in the form
4. **Keyboard shortcut matrix** — 30 min — Ctrl+S, Ctrl+D, Ctrl+/ help modal
5. **Add auto-summary + forecast UI to Sessions and Graph** — 40 min — closes the wishlist gaps

sessions.summary: GPU-aware abort signal replaces all fixed AI inference timeouts. Created shared server/lib/gpuSignal.ts that monitors nvidia-smi every 15s: as long as GPU util > 30% or power > 50W, the request stays alive. Only aborts when GPU idle for 60s (inference finished/failed) or after 1h absolute max. This solves the fundamental problem: local AI inference time depends on context × offload × model size (30s to 40+ minutes), so no fixed timeout works. Applied to overseer.ts ask() and autoSummary.ts askAI(). Also added /api/overseer/code-audit endpoint that reads the full codebase and sends to local AI, and bumped Express body limit to 2MB.
decisions: GPU utilization + power draw is the signal for AI inference liveness, not wall-clock time. The GPU IS the sensor.
Shared utility in server/lib/gpuSignal.ts — used by overseer and autoSummary, extensible to any future AI endpoint
Idle threshold 60s (4 consecutive idle readings), absolute max 1 hour. nvidia-smi failures treated as 'active' to avoid killing on transient errors.
Express body limit bumped to 2MB to support large code-audit payloads sent via /ask/start

ledger.context: Biologically plausible (brains don't do backprop), runs entirely in GLSL compute shaders without needing PyTorch/libtorch, produces weirder and more emergent behavior than gradient-trained nets, no backward pass means massive parallelism
alternatives: Option A: PyTorch via libtorch GDExtension — rejected for build complexity and less interesting dynamics. Option B: Evolutionary strategies — considered as alternative

ledger.decision: Noosphere uses Godot 4.6.1 compute shaders via RenderingDevice API instead of raw CUDA
ledger.context: Cross-platform Vulkan, built-in renderer, UI, audio, scene tree, and input handling — we only need to write the GLSL. 8GB VRAM on RTX 3070 Ti is far more than the ~14MB compute budget, so rendering and compute coexist cleanly. Hot-reload potential for shaders. No external build pipeline.
alternatives: Option A: Raw CUDA + custom renderer — rejected
months of infrastructure work. Option B: Python + CuPy + pygame — rejected
too slow for real-time rendering of field. Option C: Unity with compute shaders — possible but Godot 4.6.1 already on machine and lighter weight.

ledger.decision: Noosphere tensor field: 64x64x32 grid with 16 channels, PDE solver via wave equation + diffusion + damping
ledger.context: Field is the communication substrate and the game's narrator. Channel layout: 0-3 semantic, 4-5 emotion, 6-7 causal, 8-9 resources, 10-11 social, 12-13 player, 14-15 memory. 8MB VRAM double-buffered for Verlet integration. Workgroup (8,8,8) dispatched over full volume.
alternatives: Option A: Smaller 32^3 field — insufficient spatial detail. Option B: Larger 128^3 — 8x memory
not needed for target 60 agents. Option C: 2D field — rejected
loses volumetric dynamics that make emergent standing waves interesting.
